compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
# Enforce release-source authorization + version lockstep before publication

Re-anchor the release to the proven OSS pattern (uv model): a single
`workflow_dispatch`-from-`main` workflow that asserts version-lockstep + ancestry,
then **builds and publishes directly** via PyPA Trusted Publishing (OIDC — no
long-lived secret, no tag relay), gated on a required-reviewers `pypi` environment
re-keyed from "v* tags only" to "`main` via dispatch". The source is reviewed-and-
landed `main` **by construction**. (Readiness Finding 1 — verified VALID against
`release.yml:17-21`, `infra/terraform-github/main.tf:23-101`, `docs/releasing.md`.)

## Why

`main` is constrained to the Gerrit replication deploy key, but `refs/tags/v*` was
deliberately left open (`infra/terraform-github/main.tf:23-28`) so a human can push
a `v*` tag to drive PyPI + MCP-registry + GitHub-Release publication
(`release.yml:17-21`, jobs `build`/`publish`/`github_release`/`mcp_registry`). The
only gate anywhere is `if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref,
'refs/tags/v')` (release.yml:53/81/116) — nothing checks that the tagged SHA is an
ancestor of (or equal to) reviewed `main`, verifies any Gerrit/CI result for that
exact SHA, or asserts version consistency. A PEP 740
attestation would correctly name this repo's workflow but cannot distinguish a tag
on reviewed `main` from a tag on any other commit. Separately, the three version
values (`pyproject.toml:10`, `server.json` top-level and `packages[].version`) are
kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook (`docs/releasing.md`), so a future
mismatch would publish disagreeing versions across the PyPI / GitHub-Release / MCP
channels with no machine check.

## What

Adopt uv's proven release process and pydantic's pre-build version check, in this
order of control strength:

- **Primary control — initiate from `main` via `workflow_dispatch` (source reviewed
  by construction).** Make `workflow_dispatch` the sanctioned release trigger, run
  from the `main` ref. Because the GitHub mirror's `main` is exactly the
  Gerrit-reviewed-and-landed history, releasing from `main` guarantees the source is
  reviewed **by construction** — the same guarantee the wider OSS world gets from
  "release only from the protected branch." This replaces "accept whatever commit an
  arbitrary pushed tag points at," which was the actual divergence from consensus.
- **Version-lockstep, before build (pydantic pattern).** A first job parses the
  intended version and every manifest and asserts exact equality **before build**:
  `version_input == project.version == server.json.version ==
  each server.json.packages[].version`; mismatch fails before build.
- **Ancestry assertion (belt-and-suspenders).** In the same dispatch job, assert the
  checked-out `main` HEAD is reachable from `origin/main` via
  `git merge-base --is-ancestor "$GITHUB_SHA" origin/main` (checkout with
  `fetch-depth: 0` + `git fetch origin main`, since a shallow clone defeats ancestry —
  the repo documents this at `reconcile-bridge.yml:83-88` and `verify-identity.yaml`
  already uses `fetch-depth: 0`). With dispatch-from-`main` this is redundant with the
  ref guard, kept as defense-in-depth.
- **Build, then publish DIRECTLY via PyPA Trusted Publishing — no tag relay.** The
  same workflow builds and then publishes using OIDC (`id-token: write`, the existing
  `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish` trusted-publishing flow). There is **no** intermediate
  tag push: a tag pushed by the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` would not trigger a downstream
  workflow anyway (GitHub's anti-recursion rule), so a two-phase relay is avoided
  entirely, and no PAT / GitHub App secret is introduced.
- **Compose with — do not regress — F3 and F4 (this story lands last).** Per the
  dependency chain (F4 → F3 → F1), this story edits `release.yml` on top of F4's and
  F3's changes. The dispatch trigger must **retain** F3's build-once → test-wheel /
  test-sdist → hash-gated-`publish` job graph and F4's `mcp_verify`/`mcp_registry`
  split + evidence-consolidation job — the trigger change swaps only *how the run
  starts and is authorized*, not the internal job structure. Concretely, **re-key
  every downstream version source and job guard from `github.ref_name` / the
  tag-context `if:` to the `workflow_dispatch` input** (the `github_release` and
  `mcp_registry` jobs derive their version from the input, and their
  `if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')` guards —
  which go always-false once the tag trigger is removed — are updated to the dispatch
  condition).
- **Human authorization — re-key the `pypi` environment + a fail-closed preflight
  (uv release-gate + PyPA Trusted Publishing).** The `publish` job runs in the `pypi`
  deployment environment with **required reviewers**, so publication needs a second
  human's approval. Because the job now runs in the dispatch (`refs/heads/main`)
  context rather than a tag context, the environment's deployment-ref policy is
  **re-keyed from "v* tags only" to "`main` via `workflow_dispatch`"** with required
  reviewers — **no new secret**. This is the single most security-critical gate
  (anyone with `actions:write` can start a dispatch; only the environment's reviewers +
  branch policy enforce "a second human approves each publish"), so it must not live
  as an un-versioned console setting that could be silently cleared. Rather than couple
  to the AWS-authed `terraform-drift.yml` (a different provider/auth model), enforce it
  **at release time**: a **preflight step queries the GitHub REST API
  (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/environments/pypi`) and FAILS the release closed** if the
  environment lacks required reviewers or its deployment-branch policy is not
  `main`-only. This detects drift exactly when it matters (it blocks the actual
  publish, stronger than a periodic plan), is codebase-verifiable (the step exists in
  `release.yml`), and needs only the workflow's own read token. Net effect: "only `v*`
  tags may publish" becomes the stronger, self-enforced "only a dispatch-from-`main`,
  reviewer-approved run may publish — and a release aborts if that protection is
  missing".
- **Tag + GitHub Release created AFTER a successful publish (record only).** Once
  publish succeeds, the workflow creates the `v*` tag and the GitHub Release for the
  record/mirror; the tag is an **output**, never a trigger. Concretely, the `github_release`
  job (which holds `contents: write`) **drops `--verify-tag`** and calls
  `gh release create "v<version>" --target "$GITHUB_SHA" --generate-notes --latest dist/*`,
  which **creates the lightweight tag at the release SHA** when it does not pre-exist — so no
  intermediate tag push / PAT is needed, and the version comes from the dispatch input, not a
  pre-pushed tag. (An existing-release run stays idempotent via the `gh release view` path.) The MCP job derives the
  version from the dispatch input (not from a tag).
- **No raw-tag publish path.** With the environment keyed to dispatch-from-`main`, a
  hand-pushed `v*` tag cannot reach the `pypi` environment and therefore cannot
  publish — this eliminates the "arbitrary pushed tag publishes" surface that was the
  original finding, rather than merely gating it.
- **OIDC subject stays environment-scoped — the PyPI trusted-publisher entry is
  unaffected by the trigger change.** The `publish` job keeps running in the `pypi`
  deployment environment, so the OIDC token's `sub` claim stays
  `repo:navapbc/rebar:environment:pypi` — **environment-scoped and ref-independent**.
  PyPA Trusted Publishing matches on owner/repo + **workflow filename (`release.yml`)
  + environment name (`pypi`)** and does **not** constrain on the git ref/tag, so the
  tag→dispatch trigger change alters none of the matched fields and the existing
  trusted-publisher entry continues to accept the dispatch-context token with no
  reconfiguration. (Were the entry ever ref-constrained, the mismatch would fail
  **loudly** at the `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish` step on the first dispatch run —
  never silently — surfacing rather than corrupting a release.)
- **Verify the guard LOGIC with unit tests (no live staging needed).** Extract the
  three guards into small testable units — the version-lockstep comparison, the
  ancestry check, and the env-preflight response parsing — and unit-test them with
  fixtures (matching/mismatching versions; ancestor/non-ancestor SHAs; env-protection
  present/absent JSON). This makes the negative paths codebase-verifiable without any
  live publish. The guard *steps existing* in `release.yml` is likewise
  codebase-verifiable.
- **Guard-firing is verified AUTONOMOUSLY on the real workflow — no staging fork.** The
  three abort guards precede every publishing job, so they can be demonstrated to fire
  WITHOUT any real publish: after this story lands, the agent triggers `release.yml` via
  `gh workflow run` with a non-`main` ref and a mismatched version input, and confirms via
  `gh run view --log` that each run aborts at its guard step before build/publish. This
  needs only the environment's `workflow` scope (present) and produces no PyPI/registry
  side effect. A staging fork + a TestPyPI trusted-publisher would only be needed to
  exercise the *positive* real-publish path, which is deliberately NOT part of verifying
  this story (publishing a real release is not a story deliverable); that positive path is
  covered by the unit-tested guard helpers + the codebase-verifiable dispatch-from-`main`
  wiring. **Live `pypi` environment config is also autonomous:** the agent reads and, if
  drifted, configures the environment's required reviewers + `main`-only branch policy via
  the admin GitHub API (the environment holds a `repo`-scoped admin token), then confirms
  — escalating to request access only if the API write is denied.
- **`main.tf` / tag-push restriction stays OUT of scope (unchanged, deliberate).**
  `main.tf:23-28` documents the `tag_lock` was removed (no viable GitHub tag bypass
  actor in this mirror topology) and the contract test
  `tests/unit/test_mirror_lock_terraform.py::test_module_is_main_only_no_tag_lock`
  codifies "no tag lock". We do not restrict *who* can push a tag; publication safety
  now comes entirely from dispatch-from-`main` + the re-keyed reviewer-gated environment.
- Record the approved SHA, version, manifest versions, and artifact hashes in release
  evidence.

## Scope

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — switch the trigger to `workflow_dispatch` (version
  input, run from `main`); enforce `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`; add the
  version-lockstep + ancestry checks before build; build then publish directly via the
  OIDC trusted-publishing flow gated on the re-keyed `pypi` environment; create the
  `v*` tag + GitHub Release after a successful publish (record only). Remove the
  `push: tags: v*` publish trigger (it can no longer reach the re-keyed environment).
- **Autonomous post-landing verification (no operator fork):** after this story lands,
  the agent (a) triggers `release.yml` via `gh workflow run` with invalid inputs and
  confirms via `gh run view` that each guard aborts before publish (needs only the
  `workflow` scope), and (b) reads/configures/verifies the live `pypi` environment's
  required reviewers + `main`-only branch policy via the admin GitHub API (`repo` scope).
  Both record their evidence as comments on this ticket. No staging fork or TestPyPI
  publisher is required; escalation is a fallback only if the needed GitHub scope is
  denied.
- Unit tests for the extracted guard helpers (version-lockstep, ancestry, env-preflight
  parsing) — the codebase-verifiable proof the guards behave correctly.
- `tests/unit/test_release_workflow_pins.py` — **extend** with fd84's structural checks
  (dispatch-only trigger with a `version` input; the `authorize` job's four guards precede
  build via `needs`; no stale tag-context `if:`/`github.ref_name` remains; the
  `github_release` job needs `publish`). The existing 08a8/6168 invariants are
  **preserved unbroken** by this story's edits: the `authorize` job holds only
  `contents: read` (no `id-token: write`), so `test_single_annotated_zizmor_suppression`
  (exactly one suppression) still holds; the publish job's hash-gate/no-rebuild body
  (`test_g_publish_hash_gated_no_rebuild`) and the mcp_verify/mcp_registry split
  (`test_mcp_verify_registry_split`) are untouched except for the tag→dispatch `if:` re-key,
  which those tests do not assert on — so they stay green (re-run the full module to confirm).
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — a **preflight step** in the dispatch flow that
  calls `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/environments/pypi` and fails the release if the
  environment lacks required reviewers or a `main`-only deployment-branch policy
  (fail-closed drift detection at release time). **Preflight schema (the exact
  field paths the fail-closed parser inspects and the unit-test fixtures replicate):**
  from `GET .../environments/pypi`, require `protection_rules[]` to contain an entry with
  `type == "required_reviewers"` **and** a non-empty `reviewers` list; and require
  `deployment_branch_policy` to be non-null with `custom_branch_policies == true`. From
  `GET .../environments/pypi/deployment-branch-policies`, require `branch_policies[].name`
  to be **exactly** `{"main"}` (any other/extra branch fails). These live in
  `scripts/release_guards.py::check_env_protection`. No `infra/terraform-github/`,
  `terraform-drift.yml`, or terraform-contract-test changes (the GitHub provider's
  AWS-vs-GitHub auth split made drift-workflow coupling disproportionate; the
  preflight is the stronger, self-contained control). `infra/terraform-github/main.tf`
  and its no-tag-lock contract test remain **unchanged**.
- `docs/releasing.md` — document the dispatch-from-`main` process, the version-lockstep
  invariant, and the required environment reviewers. **Section ownership:** this story
  owns the "Release-source authorization" section **and rewrites the now-obsolete
  existing sections it invalidates** — the "Tag → PyPI publishes automatically"
  procedure (replaced by dispatch-from-`main`) and the "Hard rules" manual-lockstep
  framing (now machine-enforced). (Finding 3 owns "Build-once" + rewriting the
  "Post-release smoke test" section; Finding 4 owns "Pinning" — so the three concurrent
  release stories do not overlap-edit the same headings.)
- Out of scope: the build-once / pinning work (Findings 3 and 4 — separate stories
  that also touch `release.yml`; coordinate ordering per the epic).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC 0 (dependency gate — verify before starting).** This story composes on top of
  F3 (build-once) and F4 (pinning), which land first per the epic chain (F4 → F3 → F1).
  Before implementation, confirm by grep that `release.yml` on the working base already
  contains F3's `wheel_test` / `sdist_test` jobs and its hash-gated `publish` (no rebuild),
  AND F4's `mcp_verify` / `mcp_registry` split + the evidence-consolidation job. If they are
  absent (F3/F4 not yet landed/merged into this branch's base), STOP and rebase onto the
  landed F3/F4 first — this story only re-keys *how the run starts and is authorized* and must
  not re-introduce or fork those job graphs. (This makes the cross-story dependency an
  explicit, checkable precondition rather than an assumed baseline.)
- [ ] The workflow **retains** F3's build-once → wheel_test/sdist_test → hash-gated `publish`
  job graph and F4's `mcp_verify`/`mcp_registry` split + evidence-consolidation job unchanged
  in structure (verified by grep: those job names still present after this story's trigger
  re-key), so the authorization change is additive, not a rewrite.
- [ ] `release.yml` is triggered by `workflow_dispatch` (version input), contains a
  guard step failing when `github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'`, a version-lockstep step,
  and an ancestry step (`git merge-base --is-ancestor`, checkout `fetch-depth: 0` +
  `git fetch origin main`), all before build; and it has **no** `push: tags` publish
  trigger — all codebase-verifiable from the workflow definition.
- [ ] The workflow **retains** F3's build-once → wheel_test/sdist_test →
  hash-gated-`publish` job graph and F4's `mcp_verify`/`mcp_registry` split +
  evidence-consolidation job, and re-keys the `github_release`/`mcp_registry` version
  sources + guards from the tag context to the `workflow_dispatch` input — verified by
  the workflow definition still containing those jobs and no `github.ref_name`/tag-`if:`
  version derivation remaining.
- [ ] The workflow publishes directly via the OIDC trusted-publishing flow (no
  intermediate tag push / no relay / no PAT) and creates the `v*` tag + GitHub Release
  only **after** a successful publish (tag is an output, not a trigger) — codebase-
  verifiable from the job graph.
- [ ] A release-time preflight step calls `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/environments/pypi`
  and fails the release closed when required reviewers are absent or the
  deployment-branch policy is not `main`-only — codebase-verifiable (the step exists in
  `release.yml`); the actual live environment configuration is set + verified
  **autonomously** via the admin GitHub API (see the environment-config AC below). So a
  cleared environment protection aborts the release instead of silently enabling
  single-actor publishing.
- [ ] Unit tests for the extracted guard helpers pass: version-lockstep flags a
  deliberate mismatch, the ancestry check rejects a non-ancestor SHA (and accepts a
  non-tip ancestor), and the env-preflight parser fails on env-protection-absent JSON —
  all codebase-verifiable without a live publish.
- [ ] The guards are demonstrated to **fire in a real `workflow_dispatch` run**,
  completed **autonomously** by the agent (no operator fork/TestPyPI needed): after this
  story lands, the agent triggers `release.yml` on `navapbc/rebar` via `gh workflow run`
  with (i) a **non-`main` ref** and (ii) a **mismatched version input**, and confirms via
  `gh run view --log` that each run **ABORTS at its guard step before any
  build/publish/tag job** — so NO PyPI / MCP-registry / GitHub-Release side effect occurs
  (the ref guard, version-lockstep, and ancestry checks all precede every publishing job).
  The run URLs + the aborting guard-step log lines are recorded as a comment on this
  ticket. The **positive** clean-dispatch → real-PyPI publish is deliberately NOT
  triggered (publishing a real release is out of scope for verifying the story; the
  guard-PASS path is covered by the unit-tested helpers + the codebase-verifiable
  dispatch-from-`main` wiring). Escalate only if `gh workflow run` / `actions:write` is
  unavailable in the environment.
- [ ] The live `pypi` GitHub environment is **configured to the codified policy —
  required reviewers enabled AND a `main`-only deployment-branch policy — and verified,
  autonomously** by the agent using the environment's admin GitHub token: it reads
  `GET /repos/navapbc/rebar/environments/pypi`; if required reviewers are absent or the
  branch policy is not `main`-only, it **configures** the environment via
  `PUT /repos/navapbc/rebar/environments/pypi` with a `reviewers` body of the shape
  `[{"type": "User", "id": <numeric-github-user-id>}]` (the API requires a **numeric**
  user/team id, not a login — resolve it with `gh api users/<login> --jq .id`; use
  `{"type": "Team", "id": <team-id>}` if a release-approvers team is preferred), set to at
  least the repo owner / a designated maintainer; `deployment_branch_policy` = custom + a `main`-only
  branch policy via the deployment-branch-policies endpoint), then **re-reads to confirm**
  compliance. The before/after API responses are recorded as a comment on this ticket,
  and the release-time preflight (the AC above) then passes against the live environment.
  Escalate only if the admin API write is denied (request repo-admin on `navapbc/rebar`).
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md` documents the dispatch-from-`main` process, the
  version-lockstep invariant, the environment re-key, and the reviewer requirement.
- [ ] `make lint`, `make typecheck`, and `make test` each pass locally (the exact
  commands CI runs, per the Makefile).
- [ ] [operator-attested] The change lands on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate
  (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`) — attested by recording the Gerrit change URL +
  the two vote outcomes/timestamps (from the Gerrit REST `changes/<n>/detail`) as a
  comment on this ticket. (This is the project's universal landing gate, satisfied
  in-session by the agent driving the change through Gerrit — no external operator.)

file_impact.reason: workflow_dispatch-from-main + version-lockstep + ancestry + env preflight; publish via OIDC; retain F3/F4 jobs
dispatch process; rewrite obsolete tag/hard-rules sections

compiled_state.title: Enforce release-source authorization + version lockstep before publication
